minimal_fix: Fixed feature data conversion in pattern pipeline
verification: Patterns now recording
Memory growing
what_worked: Preserving features array through pipeline

symptom: Pattern memory was not growing despite hours of trading. The bot only showed the BASE_PATTERN despite processing hundreds of trades.
root_cause:
The pattern recording pipeline lost features data when converting from pattern objects to the recordPatternResult API:

1. **analyzePatterns()** creates patterns with `features` field (numeric array)
2. **recordPatternResult()** was called with `signature` field (truncated string)
3. **recordPattern()** expects features array, not signature string
4. Validation failed on signature string, patterns never stored
minimal_fix: Fixed feature data conversion in pattern pipeline
verification: Patterns now recording
Memory growing
what_worked: Preserving features array through pipeline

symptom: - Three different sources of "truth" tracking the same data:
root_cause:
- Three different sources of "truth" tracking the same data:
  - `this.currentPosition` in main bot (run-empire-v2.js)
  - `this.tradingBrain.position` in OptimizedTradingBrain
  - `this.executionLayer.positions` Map in AdvancedExecutionLayer
- Each module updating its own copy → phantom trades, balance mismatches
- "3 truths = 0 truth" - complete state chaos
verification: StateManager is now the ONLY source of truth
No more phantom trades or balance mismatches
All modules read/write to same centralized state
what_worked: StateManager is now the ONLY source of truth
No more phantom trades or balance mismatches
All modules read/write to same centralized state

symptom: - TRAI (AI decision system) blocked main loop for 2-5 seconds per decision
root_cause:
- TRAI (AI decision system) blocked main loop for 2-5 seconds per decision
- During volatile moves, bot was blind while waiting for AI to "think"
- Flash crashes could happen while bot waited for philosophical analysis
verification: Bot never waits for TRAI decisions
Can react instantly to market moves
TRAI does post-trade learning only
what_worked: Bot never waits for TRAI decisions
Can react instantly to market moves
TRAI does post-trade learning only
Mathematical logic drives real-time decisions

root_cause:
- StateManager used Maps for activeTrades
- JavaScript Maps can't be serialized to JSON
- On restart, all active trades were lost
- Bot had amnesia after every restart
verification: Active trades persist across restarts
Bot remembers exact position after crash
No more lost trades on reboot
what_worked: Active trades persist across restarts
Bot remembers exact position after crash
No more lost trades on reboot
Automatic save after every state update

symptom: - kraken_adapter_simple.js works but doesn't implement IBrokerAdapter interface
root_cause:
- kraken_adapter_simple.js works but doesn't implement IBrokerAdapter interface
- System expects v2 architecture with 30+ standard methods
- "Running a Ferrari off a matchbox car engine"
verification: Full IBrokerAdapter compliance
Position tracking via StateManager
Account polling (compensates for no private WebSocket)
what_worked: Full IBrokerAdapter compliance
Position tracking via StateManager
Account polling (compensates for no private WebSocket)
Working solution now, native rewrite later

symptom: - Rate limiter used recursion for retries
root_cause:
- Rate limiter used recursion for retries
- On 429 errors, created infinite promise chain
- Stack overflow after ~1000 rate limit hits
- Memory leak from accumulated promises
verification: No more stack overflow on rate limits
No more promise accumulation/memory leak
Clean queue-based retry mechanism
what_worked: No more stack overflow on rate limits
No more promise accumulation/memory leak
Clean queue-based retry mechanism
Processes queue every 100ms when active

symptom: Bot buying on bearish/hold brain signals - ~50% of positions opened against brain direction
root_cause: makeTradeDecision() line 1908 only checked `pos === 0 && totalConfidence >= minConfidence` without verifying brainDirection === 'buy'. Brain could say 'sell' or 'hold' and bot would still open a long position.
minimal_fix: Added `&& brainDirection === 'buy'` to BUY condition at run-empire-v2.js:1908
verification: Backtest pending - expect ~50% fewer trades but much higher win rate

symptom: MaxProfitManager tiered profit exits completely dead - exit_partial action silently ignored
root_cause: run-empire-v2.js:2082 only checked `profitResult.action === 'exit' || profitResult.action === 'exit_full'`. MaxProfitManager returns 'exit_partial' for tier hits (line 440) - this action was silently dropped.
minimal_fix: Added `|| profitResult.action === 'exit_partial'` to the exit check at run-empire-v2.js:2082. Also passes exitSize in return for partial position closing.
verification: Backtest pending - expect tiered exits to fire on profitable positions

symptom: Fees never deducted from balance - P&L overstated by ~108%, backtest results unreliable
root_cause: StateManager.openPosition() and closePosition() moved USD in/out without deducting Kraken trading fees (0.26% per side). All P&L calculations were fee-blind.
minimal_fix: Added 0.26% fee deduction on both entry (openPosition line 316) and exit (closePosition line 400) in StateManager.js
verification: Backtest pending - expect lower raw P&L but accurate real-world numbers

symptom: holdTime calculations used Date.now() instead of candle timestamps - all hold times ~0 in backtest, time-based exits broken
root_cause: 6 locations in run-empire-v2.js used Date.now() for entryTime recording and holdTime/holdDuration calculations. In backtest mode, Date.now() is wall clock time not simulated candle time, so hold durations were microseconds instead of minutes. Time-based exits (30min stale trade, min hold time) were completely inverted.
minimal_fix: Replaced Date.now() with `this.marketData?.timestamp || Date.now()` at lines 2090, 2134, 2320, 2432, 2522, 2528. Passed candle timestamp via context to StateManager.openPosition().
verification: Backtest pending - expect correct holdTime values, 30min stale exit to fire properly

symptom: Breakeven stop triggered guaranteed losses - fee buffer 0.1% vs actual 0.32% round-trip fees
root_cause: MaxProfitManager.js:730 set feeBuffer = 0.001 (0.1%). Kraken round-trip fees are 0.52% (0.26% x 2 sides). 'Breakeven' stop was actually locking in a -0.22% loss every time it triggered.
minimal_fix: Changed feeBuffer from 0.001 to 0.0035 at MaxProfitManager.js:730
verification: Backtest pending - expect breakeven stops to actually break even

symptom: Default exit thresholds (SL=1.5%, TP=2.0%, Tiers 0.5%/0.8%/1.2%) causing bleeds in ranging markets
root_cause: Stop loss too tight (1.5%) getting clipped before price could breathe. Profit tiers too aggressive, cutting winners short.
minimal_fix: Config D validated: STOP_LOSS_PERCENT=2.0, TAKE_PROFIT_PERCENT=2.5, TIER1_TARGET=0.007, TIER2_TARGET=0.010, TIER3_TARGET=0.015
verification: 6 configs tested across 3 market segments (uptrend, range, chop)
Config D: +0.05% avg vs Config A baseline -0.07%
Wider 2.0% SL lets trades breathe, tighter tiers cut winners
what_worked: Round-robin testing across segments
MFE/MAE tracking showed 2% SL optimal
what_failed: Config F (tighter tiers 0.4%/0.6%/0.8%) - cut winners short

symptom: Low confidence trades bleeding fees. Signal generator had implicit 40% floor, testing 25-60% thresholds.
root_cause: MIN_TRADE_CONFIDENCE=25% let garbage signals through. 60% starved trades in uptrends.
minimal_fix: MIN_TRADE_CONFIDENCE=0.50 (50%) - filters garbage without starving good signals
verification: Tested 25%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60% thresholds
50%: 41 trades, +0.09% avg (winner)
60%: 23 trades, +0.01% avg (starved)
25%: 57 trades, +0.05% avg (too many garbage)
what_worked: 50% threshold killed seg_5 bleed while keeping uptrend trades
what_failed: 30% and 40% identical to 25% due to signal generator floor at 40%

symptom: Need to confirm tuned config doesn't overfit to training data
root_cause: Config tuned on 2024 segments could be overfit. Needed out-of-sample validation.
minimal_fix: Validated on 2023 data (unseen) and full 45K dataset
verification: Out-of-sample 2023: 4000 candles, $16.5K→$41.5K, +0.05%, 23 trades, 69.6% WR
Full 45K: +0.73%, 289 trades, 63.7% WR, 0.92% max DD
Config generalized across regimes - not overfit
what_worked: Out-of-sample validation proved generalization

symptom: Production bot backtest shows confidence=0 on ALL signals, brainDirection=hold, zero trades executing
root_cause: StrategyOrchestrator has 9 strategies but most need specific conditions (EMA crossovers, S/R bounces, liquidity sweeps). In trending data where price stays above MAs, no strategy fires. minStrategyConfidence=0.35 filters everything.
minimal_fix: PENDING - Options: 1) Add simpler momentum strategy 2) Lower threshold for backtest 3) Use tuning-backtest.js results instead
verification: Tested with seg_16_uptrend: EMA sig=neutral, MA sig=neutral, orchestrator returns 0 strategies
what_worked: tuning-backtest.js works because it uses simpler RSI-based logic
what_failed: Production orchestrator too selective for backtest data
evidence: backtest-full-tuned.txt shows confidence:0 on all TRADE_DECISION logs

symptom: Pattern bank shows 8181 patterns when we deleted them. Two different pattern files exist.
root_cause: Two pattern storage locations: 1) trai_brain/learned_patterns.paper.json (4 patterns, clean) 2) data/pattern-memory.paper.json (8181 patterns, bloated). Bot loads from data/ not trai_brain/.
verification: rm data/pattern-memory.paper.json data/pattern-memory.paper.backup-*.json
evidence: ls -la shows both files exist with different sizes

symptom: Bot crash loop after restart - Cannot find module kraken_adapter_simple, TierFeatureFlags, OgzTpoIntegration
root_cause: Files incorrectly archived as dead code. Static analysis missed dynamic dependencies via ModuleAutoLoader.get() and IBroker wrapper chain. Bot ran on stale memory for 16h.
fix: Restored files from archive: kraken_adapter_simple.js (root), TierFeatureFlags.js (root), OgzTpoIntegration.js (core/)
what_worked: Restore from archive, not rewrite
lesson: NEVER archive without Bombardier dynamic dependency check. IBroker wrappers hide actual implementations. ModuleAutoLoader.get() is invisible to static analysis.

symptom: PAPER_TRADING=true in .env but bot sent real orders to Kraken, resulting in $50 loss
root_cause: OrderExecutor.js only checked BACKTEST_MODE, ignored PAPER_TRADING flag entirely. Orders went to real exchange despite paper mode being enabled.
minimal_fix: Added PAPER_TRADING constant and check in OrderExecutor.js line 24-25 and 105-106
verification: Bot shows 'PAPER MODE: Simulating order' when PAPER_TRADING=true
No real orders sent to exchange
what_worked: Adding explicit PAPER_TRADING check alongside BACKTEST_MODE
what_failed: Trusting that flag existed without verifying code enforced it
evidence: PM2 logs showed real Kraken orders despite PAPER_TRADING=true
lesson: ALWAYS trace flags through to enforcement point. Existence != enforcement.
